name: WEL User Added to Privileged Global Security Group
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.channel = 'Security' AND winEventLog.id = 4728 AND ((winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetUserName matches ('Domain Admins', 'Schema Admins', 'Enterprise Admins', 'DnsAdmins', '^Admin')) OR (winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetSid matches '^S-1-5-21-' winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetSid matches '-(512|518|519|544|1102)$')) AND NOT (winEventLog.data.event.eventData.subjectUserName matches '\\$$')

description: Detects the addition of a new member to the DnsAdmins group in Active Directory. Monitoring this activity is critical because members of the DnsAdmins group have control over the DNS service and can potentially execute malicious code with elevated privileges. This group's privileges make it a common target for attackers seeking to escalate privileges and maintain persistence within an environment. Extended Windows Event Log collection must be enabled for this rule to work properly.
name: WEL User Addition to DnsAdmins Group
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.data.event.system.eventID = 4732 AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetUserName = 'DnsAdmins'

description: Detects removal of users (4729) from the Administrators group to identify potential unauthorized privilege reduction or security policy changes. Extended Windows Event Log collection must be enabled for this rule to work properly.
name: WEL User Removed as Admin from Global Security Group
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.channel = 'Security' AND winEventLog.id = 4729 AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetUserName matches '^Admin'

description: Detects removal of users (4733) from the Administrators group to identify potential unauthorized privilege reduction or security policy changes. Extended Windows Event Log collection must be enabled for this rule to work properly.
name: WEL User Removed as Admin from Local Security Group
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.channel = 'Security' AND winEventLog.id = 4733 AND (winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetSid='S-1-5-32-544' OR winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetUserName matches '^Admin') and not (winEventLog.data.event.eventData.subjectUserName matches '\\$$')

description: Detects suspicious registry modifications that enable the storage of clear text credentials in Windows by setting the UseLogonCredential value to 1 in the WDigest registry path. Extended Windows Event Log collection must be enabled for this rule to work properly.
name: WEL WDigest UseLogonCredential Enabled
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.id = '4657' AND winEventLog.description.objectName matches 'System\\\\CurrentControlSet\\\\Control\\\\SecurityProviders\\\\WDigest$' AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.objectValueName in:anycase ('UseLogonCredential') AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.newValue in ('0x1', '0x00000001', '1')

description: Detects when a default local account, such as the DefaultAccount (also known as the Default System Managed Account or DSMA), is enabled. This account is typically disabled by default and is used for processes that are multi-user aware or user-agnostic. Enabling this account could indicate an attempt to exploit its default settings for malicious purposes, potentially leading to unauthorized access or privilege escalation. Extended Windows Event Log collection must be enabled for this rule to work properly.
name: WEL Windows Default Account Enabled
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.id = 4722 AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.targetUserName = 'DefaultAccount'

description: Detects Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) blocked outbound connection events (Event ID 5157) involving common EDR and antivirus agent processes. Such activity may indicate attempts to suppress or silence endpoint security tooling by applying malicious WFP filters, a technique commonly associated with EDR evasion tools such as EDRSilencer.
name: WEL Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) Blocked Outbound Traffic from EDR Agent Binary
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.id = 5157 AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.newProcessName matches ('\\\\MsSense\\.exe$', '\\\\SenseIR\\.exe$', '\\\\SenseCncProxy\\.exe$', '\\\\SenseNdr\\.exe$', '\\\\MsMpEng\\.exe$', '\\\\NisSrv\\.exe$', '\\\\CSFalconService\\.exe$', '\\\\CylanceSvc\\.exe$', '\\\\elasticagent\\.exe$', '\\\\elasticendpoint\\.exe$', '\\\\SentinelAgent\\.exe$', '\\\\TaniumClient\\.exe$')

description: Detects when the Windows Vulnerable Driver Blocklist is set to disabled. This feature is critical for preventing the loading of drivers with known security flaws that can be exploited for privilege escalation, kernel-level persistence, or security product evasion. Disabling the blocklist may indicate an attempt to bypass security controls and enable the use of malicious or vulnerable drivers, a technique commonly associated with Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) bypass and Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks. This rule is relevant for systems that support the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist, including Windows 10 version 1903 and later and Windows Server 2022 and later.
name: WEL Windows Vulnerable Driver Blocklist Disabled
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' AND winEventLog.id = '4657' AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.objectName contains '\\Control\\CI\\Config\\VulnerableDriverBlocklistEnable' AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.newValue in ('0', '0x0', '0x00000000') AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.oldValue in ('0x1', '0x00000001', '1')

description: Detects changes to the Windows registry that disable command-line auditing by setting the ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled value to 0. Attackers may modify this registry key to turn off command-line auditing, as part of their efforts to evade detection and obscure their actions during malicious activities.
name: Windows Command Line Auditing Disabled via Registry
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' AND endpoint.os = 'windows' AND event.type in ('Registry Key Create', 'Registry Value Modified') AND registry.keyPath matches '\\\\Software\\\\Microsoft\\\\Windows\\\\CurrentVersion\\\\Policies\\\\System\\\\Audit\\\\ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled$' AND registry.value = '0' AND NOT(src.process.user contains ('SYSTEM', 'AUTORITE NT', 'SISTEMA'))

description: Detects modifications to the Windows Default Domain Group Policy Object performed through the Group Policy Management Editor. Such changes can globally impact authentication, security baselines, and user or computer configurations across the domain, making this a high-value target for adversaries with elevated privileges. This activity is often legitimate during domain administration or maintenance, but unexpected or out-of-pattern changes may indicate malicious domain compromise or privilege abuse.
name: Windows Default Domain GPO Modification via Group Policy Management Editor
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and (src.process.image.path matches '\\\\mmc\\.exe$' or src.process.image.originalFileName = 'MMC.exe') and src.process.cmdline contains 'gpme.msc' and src.process.cmdline contains 'gpobject:' and src.process.cmdline contains ('31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9','6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9')

description: Detects changes to the Windows Default Domain Group Policy Object, which governs baseline security and configuration settings for all domain-joined systems. Unauthorized or unexpected modification of this GPO can enable broad privilege escalation, weaken security controls, or establish domain-wide persistence for an attacker. Such activity is often associated with domain compromise scenarios, though it may also occur during legitimate domain-wide administrative maintenance or policy updates.
name: Windows Default Domain Group Policy Object Modification
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Windows Event Logs' and winEventLog.channel = 'Security' and winEventLog.id = 5136 AND winEventLog.data.event.eventData.objectClass = 'groupPolicyContainer' and winEventLog.data.event.eventData.objectDN matches ('^CN={31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9},CN=POLICIES,CN=SYSTEM', '^CN={6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9},CN=POLICIES,CN=SYSTEM') and not (winEventLog.data.event.eventData.subjectUserName = 'SYSTEM')

description: Detects the removal of the Windows Defender context menu handler from the Windows Registry using reg.exe or PowerShell. This activity may indicate an attempt to weaken endpoint security controls or evade detection by disabling quick access to Windows Defender features. Adversaries may leverage this technique to reduce the likelihood of their actions being discovered or interrupted by security tools. Such behavior is consistent with defense evasion tactics observed in targeted attacks.
name: Windows Defender Context Menu Handler Registry Removed
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe', 'powershell_ise.exe', 'reg.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'pwsh', 'PowerShell ISE', 'Registry Console Tool')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('del', 'Remove-Item', 'ri ') or cmdScript.content contains ('del', 'Remove-Item', 'ri ')) and (src.process.cmdline contains '\\shellex\\ContextMenuHandlers\\EPP' or cmdScript.content contains '\\shellex\\ContextMenuHandlers\\EPP')

description: Detects execution of commands leveraging the 'SystemSettingsAdminFlows' LOLBin to disable Windows Defender. This behavior is indicative of adversaries attempting to weaken endpoint security controls by abusing legitimate system utilities. Such activity aligns with common defense evasion tactics, enabling threat actors to bypass protections and facilitate further malicious actions. Monitoring for this technique is critical, as it may precede additional compromise or lateral movement within the environment.
name: Windows Defender Disable via LOLBin SystemSettingsAdminFlows
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor')) and (src.process.cmdline contains 'Defender' and src.process.cmdline matches:matchcase 'DisableEnhancedNotifications\\s+1|RTP\\s+1|SpynetReporting\\s+0|SubmitSamplesConsent\\s+0') or (cmdScript.content contains 'Defender' and cmdScript.content matches:matchcase 'DisableEnhancedNotifications\\s+1|RTP\\s+1|SpynetReporting\\s+0|SubmitSamplesConsent\\s+0')

description: Detects the execution of a command that adds an exclusion path to Microsoft Defender using PowerShell, specifically targeting servicing paths. The command targets directories associated with or abused by known malicious software. Monitoring such activity is crucial for identifying potential threats and preventing unauthorized modifications to security settings.
name: Windows Defender Exclusion for Servicing Path via PowerShell
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Set-MpPreference','Add-MpPreference') and src.process.cmdline contains '-ExclusionPath' and src.process.cmdline contains '\\windows\\servicing') or (tgt.process.cmdline contains ('Set-MpPreference','Add-MpPreference') and tgt.process.cmdline contains '-ExclusionPath' and tgt.process.cmdline contains '\\windows\\servicing')

description: Detects the use of 'Add-MpPreference' or 'Set-MpPreference' commands via PowerShell or Command Prompt to add a Windows Defender exclusion path targeting the 'Public' folder. This activity is observed through process creation or command script events on Windows endpoints. Attackers often abuse Defender exclusions to bypass antivirus scanning of commonly accessible directories, enabling malware persistence or data staging. The rule triggers when the exclusion path specifically references the public user folder, which is unusual in standard administrative operations. Analysts should investigate the source process, user context, and any subsequent file activity in the excluded directory.
name: Windows Defender Exclusion Path Set for Public Folder via Command Line
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Add-MpPreference', 'Set-MpPreference') or cmdScript.content contains ('Add-MpPreference', 'Set-MpPreference')) and (src.process.cmdline contains '-ExclusionPath' or cmdScript.content contains '-ExclusionPath') and (src.process.cmdline matches:anycase '[a-zA-Z]:\\\\users\\\\public' or cmdScript.content matches:anycase '[a-zA-Z]:\\\\users\\\\public')

description: Detects the execution of a batch script via the Windows Command Prompt that attempts to impair or disable Windows Defender. Adversaries may use batch scripts to execute commands that modify Windows Defender settings, disable real-time protection, or uninstall the service for defense evasion.
name: Windows Defender Impairment via Batch Script

description: Detects the use of command-line utilities such as 'powershell.exe' or 'cmd.exe' to disable or weaken Windows Defender security features via 'Set-MpPreference' or 'Add-MpPreference' commands. This rule identifies process or script executions that set critical Defender options to insecure values, such as disabling real-time monitoring, script scanning, or other protections. Adversaries and malware often use these techniques to evade detection and reduce endpoint defenses. Monitoring these activities helps identify attempts to tamper with or bypass built-in Windows security controls.
name: Windows Defender Security Features Disabled via Command Line
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.parent.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe', 'mshta.exe', 'cscript.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'explorer.exe') or src.process.parent.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor', 'Microsoft (R) HTML Application host', 'Microsoft ® Console Based Script Host', 'Microsoft ® Windows Based Script Host', 'Windows Explorer')) and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Set-MpPreference','Add-MpPreference') or cmdScript.content contains ('Set-MpPreference','Add-MpPreference')) and (src.process.cmdline contains '$true' or cmdScript.content contains '$true') and (src.process.cmdline contains ('SubmitSamplesConsent Never', 'DisableScriptScanning', 'DisableRealtimeMonitoring', 'DisableCloudProtection', 'DisableAntiSpyware',  'DisableRemovableDriveScanning', 'DisableRestorePoint', 'MAPSReporting Disabled', 'DisableScanningNetworkFiles', 'PUAProtection Disabled', 'DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem', 'DisableIOAVProtection', 'DisableBehaviorMonitoring', 'DisableCatchupFullScan', 'DisableBlockAtFirstSeen', 'DisableArchiveScanning') or cmdScript.content contains ('SubmitSamplesConsent Never', 'DisableScriptScanning', 'DisableRealtimeMonitoring', 'DisableCloudProtection', 'DisableAntiSpyware', 'DisableRemovableDriveScanning', 'DisableRestorePoint', 'MAPSReporting Disabled', 'DisableScanningNetworkFiles', 'PUAProtection Disabled', 'DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem', 'DisableIOAVProtection', 'DisableBehaviorMonitoring', 'DisableCatchupFullScan', 'DisableBlockAtFirstSeen', 'DisableArchiveScanning')) and src.process.integrityLevel != 'SYSTEM' and not (src.process.cmdline contains 'Get-MpComputerStatus')

description: Detects modification of the Windows Defender Tamper Protection registry value. This activity may indicate an attempt to disable or weaken security controls, as adversaries often target Tamper Protection to facilitate further malicious actions. Unauthorized changes to this setting can expose endpoints to increased risk by allowing the manipulation of Defender configurations. Such behavior aligns with common defense evasion techniques observed in targeted attacks.
name: Windows Defender Tamper Protection Registry Value Modified
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Registry Value Modified' and registry.valueType = 'DWORD' and registry.keyPath = 'MACHINE\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Features\\TamperProtection' and registry.value = '4'

description: Detects changes to Microsoft Defender's threat severity default action via PowerShell where the action is set to 'Allow' or 'NoAction'. Such modifications can weaken endpoint protection by permitting potentially malicious files or activities to proceed without remediation. Adversaries may leverage this technique to bypass security controls and maintain persistence within an environment. This behavior is indicative of attempts to disable or circumvent endpoint defenses using native Windows tools.
name: Windows Defender Threat Action Downgrade via PowerShell
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor')) and (src.process.cmdline contains 'Set-MpPreference' or cmdScript.content contains 'Set-MpPreference') and (src.process.cmdline contains ('-LowThreatDefaultAction', '-ModerateThreatDefaultAction', '-HighThreatDefaultAction', '-SevereThreatDefaultAction', '-ltdefac ', '-mtdefac ', '-htdefac ', '-stdefac ') or cmdScript.content contains ('-LowThreatDefaultAction', '-ModerateThreatDefaultAction', '-HighThreatDefaultAction', '-SevereThreatDefaultAction', '-ltdefac ', '-mtdefac ', '-htdefac ', '-stdefac ')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Allow', '6', 'NoAction', '9') or cmdScript.content contains ('Allow', '6', 'NoAction', '9'))

description: Detects attempts to uninstall Windows Defender using PowerShell, which may indicate an effort to disable critical security features on a Windows endpoint. This activity is significant as it can be part of a broader attack strategy aimed at weakening system defenses. The detection rule identifies such attempts by monitoring process creation events where 'powershell.exe' is used with command lines containing 'Uninstall-WindowsFeature' or 'Uninstall-Feature' and 'Windows-Defender'. By alerting on these events, the rule helps maintain system integrity and prevent unauthorized security modifications.
name: Windows Defender Uninstallation via PowerShell
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Process Creation', 'Command Script') and (src.process.parent.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe', 'cmd.exe', 'mshta.exe', 'cscript.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'explorer.exe') or src.process.parent.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell', 'Windows Command Processor', 'Microsoft (R) HTML Application host', 'Microsoft ® Console Based Script Host', 'Microsoft ® Windows Based Script Host', 'Windows Explorer')) and (src.process.name in:anycase ('powershell.exe') or src.process.displayName in:anycase ('Windows PowerShell')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Uninstall-WindowsFeature','Uninstall-Feature') or cmdScript.content contains ('Uninstall-WindowsFeature','Uninstall-Feature')) and (src.process.cmdline contains ('Windows-Defender') or cmdScript.content contains ('Windows-Defender')) and not (src.process.user matches:anycase '\\\\SYSTEM$|\\\\SISTEMA$|\\\\Système$|\\\\NETWORK SERVICE')

description: Detects modification of Windows Defender 'WdFilter' and Tamper Protection registry keys. Such changes may indicate attempts to disable or weaken built-in security controls, enabling adversaries to evade detection and maintain persistence. Threat actors often target these registry settings to bypass Windows Defender protection mechanisms as part of defense evasion tactics. Unauthorized alterations to these keys can significantly reduce the effectiveness of Windows Defender, increasing the risk of further compromise.
name: Windows Defender WdFilter and Tamper Protection Registry Alteration

description: Detects modification attempts to a Windows Defender WdFilter Anti-Tamper registry key. Such activity may indicate an adversary's effort to disable or weaken Windows Defender protections, facilitating further malicious actions. Tampering with this registry setting is a known first step in the process to disable Windows Defender Real Time Protection.. This behavior aligns with adversary patterns seeking to impair endpoint defenses and maintain unauthorized access.
name: Windows Defender WdFilter Anti-Tamper Registry Modification
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Registry Value Create', 'Registry Value Modified', 'Registry Value Delete') and registry.keyPath = 'MACHINE\\SYSTEM\\ControlSet001\\Services\\WdFilter\\Instances\\WdFilter Instance\\Altitude' and registry.value != '328010' and not (src.process.displayName in ('Primitive Operations Queue Executor', 'Windows Modules Installer Worker') or (src.process.parent.displayName = 'Windows® installer' and src.process.displayName = 'Windows® installer'))

description: Detects the disabling of the Windows Defender WMI Autologger session through the use of reg.exe. This activity may indicate an attempt to impair security monitoring and hinder forensic analysis by adversaries. Threat actors may leverage this technique to reduce the visibility of their actions and evade detection by endpoint security solutions. Disabling security logging components is a known tactic to facilitate stealthy operations on compromised hosts.
name: Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session Disabled via Reg.exe
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Registry Value Create', 'Registry Value Modified') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('reg.exe') or src.process.displayName = 'Registry Console Tool') and (registry.keyPath matches 'MACHINE\\\\SYSTEM\\\\CurrentControlSet\\\\Control\\\\WMI\\\\Autologger\\\\DefenderApiLogger\\\\Start$' or registry.keyPath matches 'MACHINE\\\\SYSTEM\\\\CurrentControlSet\\\\Control\\\\WMI\\\\Autologger\\\\DefenderAuditLogger\\\\Start$') and registry.value = '0'

description: Detects execution of 'wermgr.exe' from directories outside its default system location. Adversaries may use renamed or relocated copies of legitimate Windows Error Reporting Manager to evade security controls and blend malicious activity with normal system processes. Unusual execution paths for 'wermgr.exe' can indicate process masquerading or attempts to bypass application whitelisting. This behavior is associated with techniques involving defense evasion and persistence.
name: Windows Error Reporting Manager Execution from Non-Standard Directory
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.name in:anycase ('wermgr.exe', 'WerFault.exe') or src.process.displayName in ('Windows Problem Reporting')) and not (src.process.image.path matches ('[a-zA-Z]:\\\\Windows\\\\System32|SysWOW64|WinSxS', 'HARDDISKVOLUME[1-9][0-9]?\\\\Windows\\\\System32|SysWOW64|WinSxS'))

description: Detects instances of explorer.exe spawning cmd.exe, accompanied by common start and exit commands often associated with various malicious activities. Adversaries can abuse this behavior to execute commands and scripts within the Windows environment, often to run system-level commands, execute malware, or perform lateral movement. By exploiting the normal interaction between explorer.exe and cmd.exe, attackers can hide their malicious actions under legitimate processes, making detection more difficult. This technique is commonly used in conjunction with other attack methods such as living off the land or privilege escalation, allowing adversaries to maintain persistence, execute payloads, or escalate privileges without triggering conventional security defenses.
name: Windows Explorer Spawning Command Shell with Start and Exit Commands
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and src.process.parent.name contains 'explorer.exe' and src.process.name in:anycase ('cmd.exe') and src.process.cmdline contains 'start' and src.process.cmdline contains 'exit' and not (src.process.cmdline contains ('streamer.exe', 'BGInfo', 'ClearBrowsingHistoryOnExit', 'RemoveMetroOnFirstLogon', 'IQcloud.bat', 'LLURM', 'goscreen.bat', 'TVSupport', 'RabbitMQ', 'lettres_reseau.bat', 'AutoIt3ExecuteScript', 'DeviceManager.exe', '.a3x', 'BacCfg.bat', 'Citrix_Clientname.bat ')) and src.process.cmdline contains ('.js', '.vbs', '.ps1', '.bat', '.cmd')

description: Detects the use of netsh.exe to disable the local Windows firewall. Attackers can exploit this technique to weaken the security posture of the system, allowing for unrestricted network communication. Disabling the firewall can facilitate lateral movement, enable command and control (C2) activity, and allow malicious traffic to flow undetected.
name: Windows Firewall Disabled via Netsh
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.name in:anycase ('netsh.exe') or src.process.displayName='Network Command Shell') and src.process.cmdline contains 'advfirewall' and src.process.cmdline contains ' state ' and src.process.cmdline contains ' off' and not (src.process.parent.cmdline contains:anycase ('\\Symantec\\Symantec Agent', '\\EmpirumAgent\\', '\\VMwareHorizonOSOptimizationTool', '\\OmnissaHorizonOSOptimizationTool'))

description: Detects Windows php-cgi process creations where the command line uses auto.prepend.file with php://input, indicative of PHP-CGI argument injection executing attacker-supplied request body code. This pattern is commonly tied to web shell deployment or remote code execution against public-facing PHP applications (ATT&CK: T1190, T1059, T1505.003).
name: Windows PHP-CGI Process Creation With Auto Prepend File And PHP Input

description: Detects remote invocation of the Windows Print Spooler service that coerces a target system to authenticate to a remote endpoint, commonly referred to as the 'PrinterBug' technique. This behavior is frequently abused for NTLM relay and credential access to facilitate lateral movement or privilege escalation. While legitimate printer management or discovery operations may resemble this pattern, such activity is uncommon outside controlled administration workflows.
name: Windows Print Spooler Authentication Coercion (PrinterBug)
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.name in:anycase ('python', 'python3') or src.process.displayName contains 'python') and src.process.cmdline matches 'printerbug.py' and src.process.cmdline contains '@'

description: Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature through registry manipulation. Windows Recall can be activated by setting the registry value `DisableAIDataAnalysis` to 0 or by deleting the existing `DisableAIDataAnalysis` entry. Adversaries may abuse this functionality to gain persistent access to sensitive user data, as the Recall feature continuously records and stores user activity for retrieval. By enabling Recall in a compromised environment, an attacker could expand their ability to discover files, capture sensitive information, and monitor user behavior for later exploitation. This action is particularly dangerous in post-exploitation scenarios, where enabling Recall provides a stealthy and automated method of collection without requiring direct interaction from the attacker. Such abuse could support credential theft, data exfiltration, or preparation for further compromise across the environment.
name: Windows Recall Feature Enabled via Registry Manipulation
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Registry Value Create', 'Registry Value Modified', 'Registry Value Delete') and registry.keyPath contains '\\Software\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI\\DisableAIDataAnalysis' and registry.value != '1' and src.process.name = * and src.process.integrityLevel != 'SYSTEM'

description: Detects registry modifications where the UseActionCenterExperience value is set to 0, which disables Windows Security Center notifications. While administrators may occasionally adjust notification settings for usability, adversaries may tamper with this registry key to suppress security alerts and hinder user awareness of malicious activity. Disabling security notifications is a defense evasion tactic that allows threats to persist undetected. Monitoring for such registry changes is essential to ensure endpoint visibility, enforce security policies, and detect attempts to impair defenses.
name: Windows Security Center Notifications Disabled via Registry
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type in ('Registry Value Create', 'Registry Value Modified') and tgt.process.name contains 'reg' and registry.keyPath contains '\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\ImmersiveShell\\UseActionCenterExperience' and registry.value = '0'

description: Detects the use of devcon.exe utility to disable VMware Virtual Machine Communication Interface (VMCI) devices on Windows virtual machines. This technique has been observed in VMware ESXi exploitation campaigns where attackers disable the VMCI device to escape VMs and execute code on the ESXi host.
name: Windows VMware VMCI Device Disablement via Devcon Utility
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and tgt.process.image.path matches:anycase '\\\\devcon\\.exe$' and tgt.process.cmdline contains:anycase ' disable ' and tgt.process.cmdline contains:anycase ('15AD&DEV_0740', 'VMWVMCIHOSTDEV')

description: Detects the loading of the WinPmem driver, a tool that provides raw access to physical memory. Adversaries use this capability to dump memory from sensitive processes, such as the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS), to steal credentials, encryption keys, and other secrets.
name: WinPMem Driver Load for dumping Physical Memory

description: Detects WinRAR file extraction activities that leverage path traversal combined with alternate data streams to write files outside the intended extraction directory. This technique can be used by adversaries to bypass directory restrictions and deposit malicious files in sensitive locations. Exploiting alternate data streams in conjunction with path traversal may indicate an attempt to evade security controls or establish persistence. Such behavior aligns with tactics used to escalate privileges or facilitate lateral movement within a compromised environment.
name: WinRAR Path Traversal File Write via Alternate Data Streams
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Behavioral Indicators' and indicator.name = 'WriteToADS' and (src.process.name in:anycase ('winrar.exe', 'winrar') or src.process.displayName in ('WinRAR archiver', 'WinRAR.exe', 'WinRAR')) and indicator.metadata contains '\\..\\'

description: Detects an application endpoint that permits unauthorized access to protected resources. This behavior may result from misconfigurations, exposed APIs, or insufficient authentication and access control mechanisms. Unauthorized endpoint access can lead to data breaches, lateral movement, persistence, and privilege escalation by adversaries. Continuous monitoring and prompt remediation of these exposures are critical to maintaining the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of application services.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint Allowing Unauthorized Access
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and unmapped.severity in ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') and analytic.name in ('Application endpoint exposing container on VM using IMDSv1 and running technology allowing unauthorized access and SSRF', 'Application endpoint exposing VM using IMDSv1 and running technology allowing unauthorized access leading to SSRF', 'Application endpoint exposing technology allowing unauthorized access', 'Application endpoint exposing a database configured to allow unauthorized access')

description: Detects an application endpoint that exposes cleartext cloud service credentials or tokens. Such exposures can result from misconfigurations, insecure coding practices, or insufficient access controls, leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation by adversaries.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint Exposing Cleartext Cloud Keys or Tokens
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity in ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') and analytic.name in ('Application endpoint exposing cleartext SaaS API tokens', 'Application endpoint exposes cleartext SAS tokens leading to an internal storage account', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities on a VM/serverless with cleartext long-term cloud keys granting high privileges', 'Application endpoint exposing cleartext cloud keys', 'Application endpoint exposing cleartext long-term cloud keys', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities detected agentlessly on a VM/serverless with cleartext long-term cloud keys granting high privileges')

description: Detects an Application Endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities leading to privileged access opportunities. Such instances pose significant security risks, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation leading to lateral movement, persistence, privilege escalation, and unauthorized access to sensitive information. Continuous monitoring and prompt mitigation of these exposures are essential to maintain the security and integrity of application endpoints.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint Exposing Privileged Access
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity in ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') and analytic.name in ('Application endpoint exposing Next.js vulnerable to CVE-2025-29927 detected agentlessly on a VM/serverless', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities detected agentlessly on a VM/serverless with a lateral movement finding to admin', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities detected agentlessly on a VM/serverless with high privileges', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities on a VM/serverless with a lateral movement finding to admin', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities on a VM/serverless with high privileges', 'Application endpoint exposing technology allowing unauthorized access on a VM/serverless with high privileges')

description: Detects an Application Endpoint exposing sensitive information either directly or indirectly via vulnerable software. Such instances pose significant security risks, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation leading to persistence, privilege escalation, and unauthorized access to sensitive information. Continuous monitoring and prompt mitigation of these exposures are essential to maintain the security and integrity of application endpoints.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint Exposing Sensitive Data Access
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity in ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') and analytic.name in ('Application Endpoint exposing sensitive data', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities detected agentlessly on a VM/serverless with data access to sensitive data', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities on a VM/serverless with data access to sensitive data', 'Application endpoint exposing technology allowing information disclosure on a VM with data access to sensitive data', 'Application Endpoint exposing technology with high severity unauthorized access Host Configuration Finding on a VM/serverless with data access to sensitive data')

description: Detects an application endpoint that exposes software with initial access vulnerabilities. Such vulnerabilities can lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation, allowing adversaries to gain footholds in the environment. Continuous monitoring and prompt remediation of these exposures are critical to maintaining the security and integrity of application services.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint Exposing Software with Initial Access Vulnerabilities
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity = 'HIGH' and analytic.name in ('Application endpoint exposing software on a PaaS resource with initial access vulnerabilities', 'Application endpoint exposing software with initial access vulnerabilities', 'Application endpoint exposing software with high/critical network vulnerabilities with a known exploit on a VM/serverless')

description: Detects an application endpoint configured with default or weak credentials. Such configurations pose significant security risks, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation by adversaries seeking to gain footholds within the environment. Default credentials are often intended for initial setup and should be changed before deployment. However, failure to update these credentials can leave systems vulnerable to unauthorized access. Weak passwords—those that are short, common, or easily guessable—further exacerbate this risk. Attackers can exploit these weaknesses to bypass authentication mechanisms, leading to access of sensitive data and systems.
name: Wiz Application Endpoint with Default or Weak Credentials
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity = 'HIGH' and analytic.name in ('Application endpoint exposing technology configured with default or weak credentials')

description: Detects the initial occurrence of instances where a cloud storage bucket contains cleartext (unencrypted) access keys or secrets that are accessible by all users within the subscription or project within a 7-Day time frame. Such exposures can result from misconfigurations, such as overly permissive access control lists (ACLs) or incorrect bucket policies, inadvertently granting broad access to sensitive credentials. Storing access keys in cleartext within accessible storage buckets poses significant security risks, including unauthorized access, privilege escalation, and potential compromise of other cloud resources.
name: Wiz Bucket Exposure with Cleartext Cloud Keys Accessible by All in Subscription
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Wiz' and event.type = 'Issues' and category_name = 'Findings' and class_name = 'Security Finding' and unmapped.severity = 'HIGH' and analytic.name in ('Bucket can be accessed by all users in the subscription and contains cleartext SaaS API keys')

description: Detects a storage bucket with cleartext cloud keys that is accessible by a publicly exposed container, VM, or serverless resource that has vulnerabilities. Publicly exposed buckets can lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation of sensitive information. Continuous monitoring and prompt mitigation of these exposures are essential to maintain the security and integrity of cloud storage resources.
name: Wiz Bucket Exposure with Cleartext Cloud Keys by Vulnerable Public Resource

Such instances present substantial security risks, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation leading to lateral movement, persistence, privilege escalation, and unauthorized access to sensitive information. description: Detects WMI queries to enumerate installed antivirus products, commonly used by adversaries to identify security software before deploying malware or exploits. This technique allows attackers to adjust their tactics to evade detection.
name: WMI AntiVirus Product Enumeration
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.name in ('cmd.exe') or src.process.displayName = 'Windows Command Processor') and src.process.cmdline contains 'Namespace' and src.process.cmdline contains 'root' and src.process.cmdline contains 'SecurityCenter2' and src.process.cmdline contains 'AntiVirusProduct'

description: Detects process creation events involving suspicious usage of WMIC that redirects output, which may indicate attempts to execute or conceal malicious activities. This technique is often used for post-exploitation activities, such as executing malicious payloads, exfiltrating data, or conducting lateral movement within the network. By redirecting output, attackers can prevent logging or masking their actions, making it harder for security systems to detect and respond to their activities.
name: WMI Exec Redirect via DLL
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' AND event.type = 'Process Creation' AND src.process.cmdline contains 'wmi.dll' AND src.process.cmdline contains '2>&1'

description: Detects the use of the WMI StdRegProv provider to modify or query the Windows Registry. An adversary can abuse this to make stealthy configuration changes, establish persistence mechanisms, or operate without spawning easily detectable processes. This technique allows attackers to manipulate registry keys to execute malicious code at system startup, disable security features, or harvest credentials while blending in with legitimate administrative and enterprise management traffic.
name: WMI StdRegProv Provider Usage
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.name in:anycase ('wmic.exe') or src.process.image.originalFileName in:anycase ('wmic.exe')) and src.process.cmdline contains 'call' and src.process.cmdline contains 'stdregprov'

description: Detects the creation of a small binary named kde, designed to maintain persistence by masquerading as a legitimate KDE desktop component. This binary parses its embedded configuration and executes the Wolfsbane backdoor as its next stage. Such activity may signify attempts to establish persistence and evade detection. Monitoring this behavior is essential for identifying potential threats.
name: Wolfsbane Launcher Binary Creation

description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task executing JavaScript initiated by wscript.exe. While wscript.exe is a legitimate Windows Script Host executable for running script files, adversaries may exploit it to schedule malicious JavaScript code for execution, thereby establishing persistence or automating unauthorized activities. Monitoring for such behavior is essential to identify and mitigate potential security threats.
name: Wscript Create Scheduled Task Suspicious Javascript Execution
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and (src.process.parent.name in:anycase ('wscript.exe') or src.process.parent.displayName = 'Microsoft ® Windows Based Script Host') and (src.process.name in:anycase ('schtasks.exe') or src.process.displayName = 'Task Scheduler Configuration Tool') and src.process.cmdline contains 'create' and src.process.cmdline contains '.js'

description: Detects persistence mechanisms established through Kali Linux distributions running on Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL). This rule identifies behavioral indicators of persistence activities originating from WSL service processes when Kali Linux is involved.
name: WSL Kali Linux Persistence Detection
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Behavioral Indicators' and indicator.category = 'Persistence' and ( src.process.name = "wslservice.exe" OR src.process.displayName='Windows Subsystem for Linux Service' ) and indicator.metadata contains:anycase 'kali-linux'

description: Detects the Windows Server Update Service (wsusservice.exe) launching an unexpected child process. This behavior is a strong indicator of post-exploitation activity following a successful exploit of a vulnerability like CVE-2025-59287.
name: WSUS Server Exploitation Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'windows' and event.type = 'Process Creation' and src.process.name in:anycase ('wsusservice.exe') and not (tgt.process.name in:anycase ('csc.exe', 'werfault.exe', 'taskhostw.exe'))

description: Detects modifications to XDG autostart files on Linux endpoints, which define applications that automatically run at user login. Unauthorized or suspicious changes to these files may indicate adversary attempts to establish persistence or execute malicious code during user sessions. Monitoring for such activity is important for detecting post-exploitation behavior and maintaining endpoint integrity.
name: XDG Autostart Files Updated
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'SentinelOne' and endpoint.os = 'linux' and event.type in ('File Modification', 'File Creation') and tgt.file.path contains ('/etc/xdg/', '.config/autostart/') and not (src.process.name in ('dpkg', 'opkg', 'rpm', 'executor', 'containerd', 'dockerd', 'buildkitd'))

description: Detects instances of anonymization activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Anonymization techniques are often used by threat actors to conceal their identity, location, or other identifying information when conducting malicious activities. Monitoring such activity is crucial for identifying and mitigating potential threats.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Anonymization Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'anonymizer' or malware.name contains ('anonymizer', 'psiphon') or http_request.url.hostname matches '\\.tor$') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where anonymization activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Anonymization techniques are often used by threat actors to conceal their identity, location, or other identifying information when conducting malicious activities. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Anonymization Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'anonymizer' or malware.name contains ('anonymizer', 'psiphon') or http_request.url.hostname matches '\\.tor$') and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances of botnet activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Botnets are networks of compromised devices controlled by threat actors to conduct various malicious activities, such as DDoS attacks, spam campaigns, and data exfiltration. Monitoring botnet activity is crucial for identifying and mitigating potential threats, protecting systems and data from compromise, and ensuring business continuity.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Botnet Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'botnet' or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('botnet callback', 'botnet protection') or malware.name contains ('Botnet', 'Danabot', 'Emotet', 'Icedid', 'Lokibot', 'Mirai', 'Qakbot', 'Trickbot')) and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where botnet activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Botnets are networks of compromised devices controlled by threat actors to conduct various malicious activities, such as DDoS attacks, spam campaigns, and data exfiltration. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting systems and data from compromise and ensuring business continuity.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Botnet Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'botnet' or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('botnet callback', 'botnet protection') or malware.name contains ('Botnet', 'Danabot', 'Emotet', 'Icedid', 'Lokibot', 'Mirai', 'Qakbot', 'Trickbot')) and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances of Cobalt Strike activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Cobalt Strike is a penetration testing tool that can be used to simulate advanced persistent threats (APTs) and conduct red team operations. Adversaries may use Cobalt Strike to establish command and control (C2) channels, execute commands, and exfiltrate data from compromised systems. Monitoring Cobalt Strike activity helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting systems and data from compromise and ensuring business continuity.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Cobalt Strike Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and malware.name contains 'cobaltstrike' and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where Cobalt Strike activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Cobalt Strike is a penetration testing tool that can be used to simulate advanced persistent threats (APTs) and conduct red team operations. Adversaries may use Cobalt Strike to establish command and control (C2) channels, execute commands, and exfiltrate data from compromised systems. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting systems and data from compromise and ensuring business continuity.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Cobalt Strike Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and malware.name contains 'cobaltstrike' and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances of credential harvesting activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). This activity includes machine-in-the-middle attacks intercepting and altering communication between two parties, stealers extracting stored passwords, and kerberoasting used to extract service account credentials from Kerberos tickets. Monitoring such activity is crucial for identifying and mitigating potential threats, thereby protecting user and service accounts.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Credential Harvesting Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and malware.name contains ('.pws.', 'kerberoast', 'pwdump', 'mimikatz', 'responder', 'rubeus') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances of Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). XSS is a web security vulnerability that allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users. These scripts can steal sensitive information, deface websites, redirect users to malicious sites, or perform other malicious actions. Monitoring XSS activity helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting users and systems from compromise and ensuring a secure browsing experience.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Cross-Site Scripting Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'cross-site' or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('Cross-site Scripting (XSS)', 'Cross-site Scripting') or malware.name contains 'xss') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). XSS is a web security vulnerability that allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users. These scripts can steal sensitive information, deface websites, redirect users to malicious sites, or perform other malicious actions. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting users and systems from compromise and ensuring a secure browsing experience.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Cross-Site Scripting Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'cross-site' or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('Cross-site Scripting (XSS)', 'Cross-site Scripting') or malware.name contains 'xss') and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances where Denial of Service (DoS) activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). DoS attacks aim to disrupt or deny access to services, applications, or networks by overwhelming them with excessive traffic, requests, or malicious activity. Monitoring blocked activity involvement helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting systems and services from disruption and ensuring business continuity.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Denial of Service Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat contains 'denial of service' or malware.name contains ('dos', 'ddos')) and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects the blocking of the OAuth application eM Client. This application facilitates exporting mailboxes to PST files, individual emails to EML files, and exporting calendar, contacts, and tasks. It also supports mass mailing and features to avoid marking emails as read or placing sent emails in the Sent folder. Historically abused in Business Email Compromise (BEC) attacks, monitoring blocked attempts ensures that any attempted use is expected and legitimate, helping identify potential BEC-related threats.
name: Zscaler Internet Access eM Client Application Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and app_name contains 'eM Client' and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) activity related to Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA) through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). GLBA DLP rules help organizations protect sensitive financial information from unauthorized disclosure, or exfiltration. Monitoring GLBA DLP activity helps protect customer data and ensure compliance with industry regulations.
name: Zscaler Internet Access GLBA Data Loss Prevention
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and unmapped.dlpengine contains 'GLBA' and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) activity related to Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). HIPAA DLP rules help organizations protect sensitive patient health information from unauthorized disclosure, or exfiltration. Monitoring HIPAA DLP activity helps protect patient data and ensure compliance with industry regulations.
name: Zscaler Internet Access HIPAA Data Loss Prevention
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and unmapped.dlpengine contains 'HIPAA' and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where malicious URL activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Malicious URLs are links that lead to websites or compromised websites hosting malware, phishing pages, suspicious redirection, drive-by downloads, exploits, or other malicious content. Clicking on such URLs can compromise user devices, steal sensitive information, or install malware. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting users and systems while ensuring a secure browsing experience.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Malicious URL Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and not (unmapped.event.threatcat contains ('adware', 'cross-site', 'spyware')) and not (risk_details contains 'benign') and not (malware.name contains ('.pua.', 'adware', 'autokms', 'fakeav', 'hacktool', 'kmsauto', 'patch', 'riskware', 'scam', 'seo-spam', 'spyware', 'sqli', 'xss')) and (risk_details contains ('malware', 'trojan', 'virus') or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('malicious content') or malware.name contains ('.rat.', 'backdoor', 'cve-', 'downloader', 'dropper', 'exploit', 'hacked', 'injection', 'malware', 'malurl', 'payload', 'redirector', 'trojan', 'virus', 'webshell', 'worm')) and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects instances of offensive cybersecurity tool usage through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Offensive tools are software used by threat actors and security teams to conduct various activities, such as penetration testing, network scanning, password cracking, and privilege escalation. Offensive tools can be used for both legitimate and malicious purposes, making it essential to monitor their activity to identify and mitigate potential threats, protect systems and data, and ensure compliance with security policies.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Offensive Cybersecurity Tool Usage
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and not (malware.name contains ('autokms', 'crack.iz', 'patch', 'kmsauto')) and malware.name contains ('bloodhound', 'brutus', 'dnscat', 'edrsilencer', 'hacktool', 'mailsniper',  'metasploit', 'nmap', 'powersploit', 'sharphound', 'sharpsuite', 'sharpview', 'winpeas') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) activity related to Payment Card Industry (PCI) through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). PCI DLP rules help organizations protect sensitive payment card information from unauthorized access, disclosure, or exfiltration. Monitoring PCI DLP activity helps protect customer data and ensure compliance with industry regulations.
name: Zscaler Internet Access PCI Data Loss Prevention
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and unmapped.dlpengine contains 'PCI' and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances of Peer-to-Peer (P2P) activity through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). P2P networks allow users to share files, resources, and services directly with one another without the need for a central server. Monitoring P2P activity helps identify and mitigate the distribution of pirated content and malware, communication with command and control, and the exfiltration of sensitive information in turn protecting data, systems, and ensuring compliance with security policies.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Peer to Peer Activity
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('Peer-to-Peer Site', 'Peer To Peer (P2P)') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where Peer-to-Peer (P2P) activity is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). P2P networks allow users to share files, resources, and services directly with one another without the need for a central server. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate the distribution of pirated content and malware, communication with command and control, and the exfiltration of sensitive information in turn protecting data, systems, and ensuring compliance with security policies.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Peer to Peer Activity Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('Peer-to-Peer Site', 'Peer To Peer (P2P)') and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects the blocking of OAuth Application PerfectData. PerfectData allows for the exfiltration of a victim's mailbox to a PST (Personal Storage Table) file, and has historically been abused by adversaries to perform BEC (Business Email Compromise). Ensuring that the attempted use of this application is expected and legitimate can help identify whether this activity is related to BEC.
name: Zscaler Internet Access PerfectData Application Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and app_name contains 'PerfectData' and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects successful access to a phishing link, potentially leading to malware downloads or the disclosure of sensitive information. Monitoring such activity is critical to identifying compromised accounts or systems and mitigating risks associated with phishing attacks.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Phishing Link Accessed
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat in:anycase ('phishing') or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('phishing') or malware.name contains 'phish') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects instances where a phishing link is blocked by Zscaler Internet Access. Phishing links are often used to deliver malware or steal sensitive information. Monitoring blocked attempts helps identify and mitigate potential threats, protecting users and systems from compromise.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Phishing Link Blocked
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and (unmapped.event.threatcat in:anycase ('phishing') or http_request.url.categories in:anycase ('phishing') or malware.name contains 'phish') and action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked')

description: Detects potential phishing attachments associated with Outlook or Office 365 through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Phishing attachments are malicious files sent via email to deceive users into downloading and executing them. These attachments can harbor malware, ransomware, spyware, or other harmful payloads. Monitoring for potential phishing attachments related to Outlook is crucial for identifying threats, thereby safeguarding users and systems from compromise and ensuring secure email communication.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Potential Outlook or Office 365 Phishing Attachment
s1ql: dataSource.name = 'Zscaler Internet Access' and http_request.url.categories = 'Webmail' and app_name contains ('Outlook', 'Common Office 365 Applications') and unmapped.filetype contains ('Windows Executable (exe, exe64, scr)', 'Batch (.cmd, .bat)', 'Compiled HTML help files', 'Windows Library (dll64, dll, ocx, sys)', 'Microsoft Installer (msi)', 'WIM files (wim)', 'Java Applet (jar, class, applet)', 'Microsoft RTF (rtf)', 'Tar (tgz, gtar, tar)', 'DMG (dmg)', 'Windows Script (wsc, wsf, wsh)', 'Windows Shortcut (lnk)', 'ISO Archive (Iso)', 'Iso Archive', 'Visual Basic Script (vbs)', 'ZIP w/Suspicious Script (js, vbs, svg, ps1, hta, cmd, lnk)', 'MS Rtf', 'RAR (rar)', 'Visual Basic Files (vb, vbe, vbp)', 'Windows PowerShell Script (ps1)') and not (action contains ('Drop', 'IPS Reset', 'Blocked'))

description: Detects potential phishing attachments associated with webmail other than Outlook or Office 365 through Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA). Phishing attachments are malicious files sent via email to deceive users into downloading and executing them. These attachments can harbor malware, ransomware, spyware, or other harmful payloads. Monitoring for potential phishing attachments related to Outlook is crucial for identifying threats, thereby safeguarding users and systems from compromise and ensuring secure email communication.
name: Zscaler Internet Access Potential Webmail Phishing Attachment
